title: For the Deaf Community
subtitle: Find interpreted events. Get support at the venue. Know your rights. Never miss out again.
metaDescription: Find BSL and ISL interpreted events across the UK and Ireland with the Performance Interpreting events app. Communication cards, festival checklists, live speech-to-text, and more.

sections.label: The PI Events App
sections.heading: Everything you need - before, during and after the event
body: The PI Events App is a free app built for Deaf and hard of hearing audiences. It's not just an events listing - it's a complete toolkit for live events, from finding interpreted shows to getting support at the venue.
Available on iOS, Android and web. Search 'Performance Interpreting Events' in the App Store or Google Play.

sections.label: Find Events
sections.heading: Search and discover interpreted events
body: Browse confirmed BSL and ISL interpreted events across the UK and Ireland. Filter by date, location, event type and language. Only events where interpreting is actually confirmed appear - no guesswork.
Get push notifications when new interpreted events are added near you, for artists and venues you follow, or for last-minute ticket releases.

items.title: Smart search
items.description: Type-ahead search across events, venues and categories with fuzzy matching - finds what you're looking for even with typos.

items.title: Check interpreting status
items.description: Enter any event name or venue to check whether sign language interpreting has been booked and confirmed.

items.title: Share and save
items.description: Generate shareable links and add events directly to your calendar with one tap.

sections.label: Request Access
sections.heading: An event you love doesn't have an interpreter?
body: You have the right to request access at live events. Following the landmark Little Mix BSL case - in which PI provided key testimony - BSL access at live events is a legal requirement under the Equality Act 2010, not a favour.
Use the app to submit a request directly. Tell us the event, date and venue and we'll contact the organiser on your behalf.

items.title: Request an interpreter
items.description: Fill in the event details and we'll liaise with the organiser to get access in place.

items.title: Know your rights
items.description: The app includes a full guide to your legal rights under the Equality Act 2010, the DDA 1995 and the Irish Equal Status Acts - in plain English, no legal jargon.

sections.label: At the Event
sections.heading: Support tools to use on the day
body: The app includes a set of tools designed to help you communicate and stay safe at live events - even when it's loud, crowded or you can't find the interpreter.

items.title: Communication cards
items.description: Pre-made cards with common phrases you can show to staff - 'I am Deaf', 'Can you repeat that?', 'Where is the BSL interpreter?' Tap to display full screen.

items.title: Food & drink order builder
items.description: Build your order with quantities and send it as text - no more shouting over the music at the bar.

items.title: Emergency information
items.description: Pre-filled emergency communication templates and venue safety procedures. Clear layout, large text, high contrast.

items.title: Live speech-to-text
items.description: Real-time speech recognition that transcribes what people say on screen. Useful for quick conversations with hearing people at the event.

items.title: Feel the Music
items.description: Converts live sound into haptic vibration feedback on your phone. Feel the bass, the beat and the energy of the performance through your device.

sections.label: Plan Ahead
sections.heading: Festival checklists and venue guides
body: Going to a festival for the first time? The app includes a comprehensive planning checklist - what to bring, accessibility tips, communication planning, booking confirmations to prepare and special considerations for Deaf attendees.
The app also provides direct links to official festival apps and venue accessibility pages so you can plan your visit with confidence.

sections.label: Share Your Experience
sections.heading: Feedback that shapes future events
body: After an event, share your experience directly through the app. Your feedback goes straight to the PI team and helps us improve the service for everyone.
You can submit written feedback or upload a video response in BSL, ISL or your preferred language. Real feedback from real attendees is how we hold venues to account and push for better access.

sections.label: Download the App
sections.heading: Get the PI Events App - free on iOS and Android
body: The PI Events App is free, updated in real time and built by the people who deliver the interpreting. Everything in one place - events, tools, rights, feedback.
Download from the App Store or Google Play, or open it in your browser at app.performanceinterpreting.co.uk.

sections.label: Forms
sections.heading: Photo, video and feedback
body: If you're happy to appear in any photos or videos (including interviews) taken during interpreted events, please let us know by filling out the media consent form below.
If you've joined us at an event, we'd love to hear how it went. Your feedback helps us shape future events and improve the experience for everyone.

items.title: Photo & Video Consent
items.description: Let us know you're happy to appear in event photography and video content. This is entirely optional and you can withdraw consent at any time.

items.title: Event Feedback
items.description: Share your experience after attending an interpreted event. It only takes a minute and helps us keep improving.
